text: Good morning, everybody.  Today, we’re beginning our symmetry, and I’m gonna turn this on and ask for volunteers to come up here and explain to me what the lines of symmetry are and why. So, Juan is using the shapes below to learn about symmetry. A says, “Write the letter of the shape that has more than one line of symmetry.”  Could somebody explain to me what a line of symmetry is? Student D?
speaker: teacher
text_arabic: صباح الخير، الجميع. اليوم، نحن نبدأ التزاوج، وسأقوم بتشغيل هذا وسأطلب من المتطوعين أن يأتوا إلى هنا ويوضحوا لي ما هي خطوط التزاوج ولماذا. لذلك، يستخدم خوان الأشكال أدناه للتعلم عن التزاوج. A يقول، كتب حرف الشكل الذي لديه أكثر من خط واحد من التزاوج.

text: A line of symmetry is like a way that you can pull the shape.

text_arabic: خط التناظر هو مثل طريقة يمكنك سحب الشكل.

text: Good.  So, if you fold the shape, it should look exactly the same on both sides.  Okay.  Can you give me an example of something that’s symmetrical, Student M?

text: A square would be symmetrical, because if you folded a square it would look exactly the same on both sides.
